notes: Property taxes and insurance materially affect true monthly ownership cost near Wright-Patterson.

crime_status.metro_shreveport_bossier: Beavercreek and Fairborn are considered very safe; Dayton urban core has standard metro crime concerns.

school_quality.on_base: No on-base schools; students attend local districts.
school_quality.off_base: Beavercreek and Oakwood School Districts are exceptionally rated.

rental_vacancy.notes: Good selection of multi-family and single-family rentals in Fairborn.

rental_metrics.notes: Good selection of multi-family and single-family rentals in Fairborn.

military_lifestyle_fit.description: Massive military and defense contractor presence; high local support for Wright-Patt.

financial_brief.affordability_summary: Strongest value is found in the Fairborn and Riverside areas.
financial_brief.buyer_opportunity: Strongest in the growing Beavercreek corridors.

market_bluf.bluf_summary: Beavercreek and Fairborn offer stable value and elite schools for military families.

summary_points: Wright-Patterson Air Force Base sits in the Beavercreek / Fairborn / Dayton housing market with base-specific affordability, commute, and neighborhood tradeoffs.
Current market trend is approximately 3.1% year-over-year, which should shape buyer leverage and pricing discipline.
Strongest value is found in the Fairborn and Riverside areas.
Neighborhood quality varies across Beavercreek, Fairborn, Centerville and other nearby corridors — citywide averages should not be the only filter.
Beavercreek and Fairborn offer stable value and elite schools for military families.
opportunities: Wright-Patterson military demand can support durable housing relevance for disciplined buyers and landlords.
Local inventory and pricing may offer opportunity for households that stress-test taxes, insurance, and commute together.
Beavercreek and nearby corridors may offer stronger family or value positioning depending on assignment length.
Assignment-length clarity can improve rent-versus-buy decisions near Wright-Patterson.
risks: Neighborhood quality, schools, commute, and monthly carrying costs vary across the Beavercreek / Fairborn / Dayton market.
Property taxes, insurance, HOA, and utilities can widen the gap between list price and true monthly payment.
Market averages can hide weak submarkets — property-level review matters.
Stretching beyond a safe housing ratio can reduce PCS flexibility.
buyer_notes: Wright-Patterson buyers should compare all-in monthly cost, not just principal and interest.
Verify school zones, commute timing, and BAH fit by exact address before signing.
Strongest in the growing Beavercreek corridors.
This market rewards disciplined buyers who test the commute during expected report hours.
seller_notes: Sellers should price against current inventory and days on market rather than older peak assumptions.
Move-in-ready presentation and realistic pricing matter when buyers have leverage.
Military-adjacent commute convenience can be a listing strength if framed clearly.
investor_angles: Military turnover near Wright-Patterson can support rental demand, but vacancy and maintenance should be modeled conservatively.
Investors should underwrite taxes, insurance, HOA, and property condition — not just acquisition price.
Practical commute corridors often outperform generic metro averages for military renter demand.
buyer_guidance: Use all-in monthly payment as the primary screen.
Compare taxes, insurance, commute, school quality, and resale flexibility before locking a neighborhood.
Do not choose housing based only on distance to Wright-Patterson.
A slightly lower purchase price can materially improve monthly PCS flexibility.
seller_guidance: Price against active inventory and days on market.
Condition and presentation matter when buyers can compare more options.
Military commute convenience can help positioning if described accurately.
landlord_notes: Military renter demand can improve occupancy durability near Wright-Patterson.
Model vacancy, repairs, insurance, and turnover conservatively.
Stable spread matters more than headline affordability alone.

base_profile.host_or_major_command: 88th Air Base Wing / Air Force Materiel Command
base_profile.primary_mission_summary: Wright-Patterson AFB is a major research, acquisition, and test installation in the Dayton metro with strong defense-industry housing demand.
base_profile.pcs_personality: Massive military and defense contractor presence; high local support for Wright-Patt.
base_profile.base_bluf: Beavercreek and Fairborn offer stable value and elite schools for military families.

user_positioning.military_family_bluf: Prioritize school-zone verification, BAH fit, commute testing, childcare access, and total monthly housing cost before signing near Wright-Patterson.
user_positioning.single_airman_bluf: Prioritize commute simplicity, affordability, rental flexibility, and access to daily services around Dayton.
user_positioning.realtor_bluf: Lead with BAH realism, commute risk, school-zone confidence, and all-in payment math for Wright-Patterson clients — not generic Beavercreek / Fairborn / Dayton stats alone.
user_positioning.investor_bluf: Rental demand near Wright-Patterson is influenced by PCS turnover, training/permanent-party mix, and practical commute corridors.

gate_selection_logic: Start with the user's likely duty location.
Identify the most realistic primary gate.
Compare the commute during the user's actual report window.
Do not choose a neighborhood based only on mileage.
commute_intelligence.user_instruction: Before signing a lease or contract, test the commute from the exact property address to the expected gate during the same time window you would normally report to work.

bah_market_reality.bah_bluf: Wright-Patterson housing decisions should compare BAH against rent, mortgage, utilities, debt, and commute. BAH is a decision filter, not a promise of affordability.

bah_market_reality.base_tab_message: Show BAH as a decision filter for Wright-Patterson, not a promise of affordability. Push users into PCSUnited financial calculators when the housing gap looks tight.

base_profile.disclaimer: PCSUnited is not an official DoD source. This Base tab is intended for public-facing PCS orientation, housing research, and relocation planning. Users should verify gate hours, access requirements, medical services, housing availability, school zones, and official procedures directly with the installation or official service provider before travel, lease signing, or home purchase.

parser_notes: This Wright-Patterson AFB file was upgraded to the Lackland-style PCSUnited schema with slug, name, state_code, profile, market_metrics, metrics, ownership_costs, costs, rental_metrics, summary_points, risks, opportunities, buyer_notes, seller_notes, investor_angles, and guidance arrays.
Beavercreek / Fairborn / Dayton is preserved as the surrounding metro/submarket context; base-specific market data was retained.
property_tax_rate and insurance_rate are normalized to decimal form for deterministic math, with percent aliases preserved for display compatibility.

This file now includes base_profile for the PCSUnited Base tab, including gate orientation, visitor control center, major services, on-base housing, recommended nearby neighborhoods, commute intelligence, BAH market guidance, PCS watchouts, and source notes.
